Widespread injuries plague both squads, offsetting Fluminense's home advantage and strong recent form (7 wins in 12 matches): key absences include Germán Cano (knee), Lucho Acosta, and Soteldo for the hosts, while visitors miss Lucas Moura, Pablo Maia, Calleri (concussion protocol), and Luciano (overload). Balanced head-to-head history—capped by Fluminense's 6-0 thrashing in November 2025—combined with São Paulo's resilient away resilience keeps the matchup tightly contested amid mutual roster disruptions over the past month.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire in the Strait of Hormuz holding since April 8, 2026, following recent US-Iran naval clashes, the United States awaits Tehran's response to its latest one-page peace proposal as of May 8, with no date set for the next diplomatic round. Prior in-person talks in Islamabad earlier this month, mediated by Pakistan, featured US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and possibly Jared Kushner alongside Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, but yielded no deal on nuclear enrichment pauses or navigation freedoms. Ongoing indirect diplomacy via Oman and Pakistan leaves trader sentiment focused on whether these mid-level envoys reprise roles or higher principals like President Trump engage, ahead of potential Beijing discussions May 14-15.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the May 19 Democratic primary for Pennsylvania's 3rd Congressional District just 11 days away, trader consensus gives state Sen. Sharif Street a 60% implied probability of winning the nomination to replace retiring Rep. Dwight Evans, ahead of state Rep. Chris Rabb at 39%, driven by Street's deep ties to the Philadelphia Democratic machine, name recognition from his family legacy and former party chair role, and recent endorsement from the American Federation of Government Employees union. Rabb has surged on superior fundraising, the Philadelphia Inquirer editorial board's April 20 backing, AOC support, and Justice Democrats' $250,000 super PAC ad buy announced May 7, yet markets favor Street's expected edge in low-turnout conditions among the district's diverse northwest Philadelphia voters. Ala Stanford's odds fell to 3% after a May 5 revelation of her $500,000 AIPAC funding prompted her debate withdrawals.

markets.question: Will Mason Miller win the 2026 NL Cy Young Award?
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes commands 38.5% implied probability as the NL Cy Young favorite, bolstered by his 5-2 record, 2.36 ERA, and a dominant eight scoreless innings with seven strikeouts against the Diamondbacks two days ago, building on his 2025 win and flirtation with a perfect game last month. Shohei Ohtani trails at 18.7% after a stellar pitching return, leading MLB with a 0.60-0.97 ERA through quality starts like seven innings recently, though Dodgers manage his dual workload. Yoshinobu Yamamoto's 16.5% reflects his Opening Day ace role and 3.09 ERA in five starts. Trader consensus weighs early-season form, innings durability, and strikeout rates amid healthy rotations.
